response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior engineer with broad full-stack, systems, and open-source experience (including Linux, D, Node.js, and a self-founded GIS platform), which aligns well with the technical and open-source aspects of the role. However, the position is an executive-level leadership role requiring minimum C1 German — the resume lists German as basic, which is a hard disqualifier for a client-facing advisory role in this market. Additionally, the career history is almost entirely individual-contributor work with no demonstrated formal team leadership, people management, or project/portfolio oversight, and UML/requirements-management expertise is not evidenced.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering experience across web, embedded, and high-throughput systems at top-tier companies
Strong open-source and Linux background, including founding and running an open-source geo-data platform
Proven mentoring/knowledge-sharing inclination through workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: German listed as basic; role requires minimum C1 for client advisory work
No formal leadership or team/project management track record for a Head of Development role
No evidence of UML, requirements management, or security/compliance advisory experience
